title: Saving D.C. Homes from Foreclosure Act: Mediation Intake Logs & Compliance Records (June–Sept 2024)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the DC Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To: claudine.sassa@dc.gov, lilah.blackstone@dc.gov

Cc: bmilhorn@csbs.org, MLiu@csbs.org, kelly.lammers@nebraska.gov, daniel.lucas@dc.gov, oag@dc.gov, tim.shirey@dc.gov, odca.mail@dc.gov, Foia.Appeals@dc.gov

SUBJECT: Expedited FOIA Request: Foreclosure Mediation Administrator Records & HCS Transmissions (June 2024 – September 2024)

To the DISB FOIA Officer:

Under the District of Columbia Freedom of Information Act (D.C. Official Code § 2-531 et seq.), I am formally requesting public records generated, received, or maintained by the DISB Foreclosure Mediation Administrator between the dates of June 1, 2024, and September 30, 2024.

This request specifically targets the mandatory checks, balances, and administrative filings required under the Saving D.C. Homes from Foreclosure Act. 

Please provide the following records:

1. General Intake and Registry Logs:
The complete intake logs, registry entries, and processing metadata of all "Notices of Default on a Residential Mortgage" (NOD) submitted to the Mediation Administrator by any of the following entities or individuals during the requested timeframe:
* McCabe, Weisberg & Conway (or any attorney representing this firm)
* Jorge Campodonico
* City Lending

(Note: I am NOT requesting the Personally Identifiable Information of other D.C. residents. DISB may redact the names and addresses of other homeowners, but the log entries proving that McCabe, Campodonico, or City Lending submitted documents to DISB must remain visible).

2. Specific Homeowner Records (Fred Gamble Jr.):
I am requesting the complete, unredacted administrative file regarding the foreclosure action initiated against Fred Gamble Jr. during this timeframe, specifically including:
* The date-stamped "Notice of Default" presented to the DISB Foreclosure Mediation Administrator regarding my property.
* The mandatory mailings, including the loss mitigation application and mediation rights notice, legally required to be sent by the DISB Mediation Administrator to Fred Gamble Jr. within three (3) days of receiving the Notice of Default.
* Any "Mediation Certificate" issued by DISB to McCabe, Jorge Campodonico, or City Lending authorizing them to proceed with a foreclosure action in D.C. Superior Court.
* All transmission logs, referrals, certificates of counseling, or correspondence sent between DISB and Housing Counseling Services (HCS) regarding Fred Gamble Jr. or the associated property.

FORMATTING AND EXPORT MANDATE:
To ensure full compliance with metadata transparency, all responsive records—especially intake logs, registry entries, and digital tracking footprints—must be produced in their native electronic format (e.g., Excel, CSV, or raw database export). Under no circumstances are these records to be flattened, stripped of metadata, or converted into image-only PDFs. 

Given the ongoing investigations into DISB's regulatory oversight and the urgent nature of the active D.C. Superior Court docket regarding this exact matter, I respectfully request an expedited processing of this FOIA.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

FRED GAMBLE JR

title: Crisis Management Meeting Internal Communication - Dunwoody

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Crisis Management Meeting Internal Communication - Dunwoody
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All emails, text messages, direct messages, calendar entries, meeting notes, or other written communications between or among any employees, officials, or representatives of the City of Dunwoody (including but not limited to the Dunwoody Police Department, City Manager's office, Mayor's office, City Council members, and Technology Department) referencing, discussing, or related to the Dunwoody Real Time Crime Center (RTCC) tour scheduled for February 12, 2026, from January 1, 2026 through the present.

This request includes but is not limited to internal communications discussing the planning, scheduling, purpose, attendees, or any follow-up actions related to that event.

For purposes of this request, "communications" includes records maintained on personal devices or personal accounts if such records were prepared, sent, or received in the conduct of public business.

Pursuant to O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(1), I request that responsive records be provided in their native electronic format with full metadata preserved, including but not limited to message headers, timestamps, sender/recipient information, and attachment metadata.

If any portion of this request is denied, I request that you:
(1) Identify each responsive record being withheld with sufficient detail to permit me to assess the basis for withholding;
(2) Cite the specific statutory exemption(s) claimed for each record or portion thereof; and
(3) Pursuant to the segregability requirement of O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72(a)(44), produce all non-exempt portions of partially exempt records with redactions clearly marked and justified on a field-by-field basis.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Burn Bags - FBI
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

FOIA REQUEST

Federal Bureau of Investigation
Records/Information Dissemination Section 

Freedom of Information Act Request

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I request access to and copies of the following records:

Requested Records

Any and all records from August , 2025 to present relating to the discovery, recovery, inventorying, preservation, transfer, review, or handling of “burn bags” or materials reportedly located within a secure room, SCIF, vault, archive area, or undisclosed records storage location within FBI facilities containing records associated with:

“Crossfire Hurricane”
the Trump–Russia investigation
the Durham investigation or Durham annex
Russiagate-related records
classified annex materials reportedly referenced by FBI Director Kash Patel
records provided or transmitted to Senator Chuck Grassley or the Senate Judiciary Committee

This request includes but is not limited to:

emails
memoranda
inventories
chain-of-custody records
photographs
preservation notices
records destruction suspensions
internal FBI communications 
records referencing “burn bags”
records discussing the existence or discovery of previously undisclosed document repositories
communications with DOJ, ODNI, CIA, NSA, or Congress regarding the records
briefing materials prepared for FBI leadership
records discussing declassification review of any recovered materials

Search Terms

Please include searches reasonably calculated to locate responsive records using terms including:

“burn bag”
“Crossfire Hurricane”
“Durham annex”
“secret room”
“SCIF”
“Grassley”
“Russiagate”
“hidden documents”
“classified annex”
“Kash Patel”

Public Interest

This request concerns matters of exceptional public interest involving alleged handling, preservation, and possible destruction of historically significant FBI and intelligence-community records tied to the origins of the Trump–Russia investigation. Media reports indicate these materials may involve classified annexes and records previously unknown to Congress or the public.

Format Requested

I request responsive records in electronic format pursuant to the E-FOIA amendments, preferably as searchable PDF files delivered via email.

Fee Waiver Request

I request a waiver of all search and duplication fees because disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of government operations and activities. I am a requester engaged in news media and public-interest research activities.

However, if fees are expected to exceed $25, please contact me before processing.

Related recent news:  https://www.dailymail.com/news/article-15795357/The-FBI-hidden-burn-bag-room-Kash-Patel.html

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

William Fernandes

Pursuant to the District of Columbia Freedom of Information Act (D.C. Code § 2-531 et seq.), I am requesting expedited access to the following public records regarding a consumer complaint I filed against City Lending, Inc. in the fall of 2019. 

The complaint was assigned DCID #2059580 and was handled by Consumer Services Specialist Shahidah S. Williams, JD, within the Compliance Analysis Division. 

On November 6, 2019, Ms. Williams stated in writing that she was administratively closing my complaint and promised that an "official letter" would be generated. However, the core issues of predatory payoff escalations and the withholding of monthly statements were never resolved by your agency.

I demand the immediate production of the following precise records generated between September 1, 2019, and December 31, 2019:

White, P.C.
3. Supervisory Authorization: All records showing supervisory review and approval authorizing Shahidah Williams to administratively close DCID #2059580.

(specifically Jorge Campodonico or Stephanie Simmons) regarding their failure to provide the consumer with monthly billing statements and their refusal to update the consumer's mailing address.
5. Escalation & Disciplinary Action (Predatory Payoff): All records, internal memoranda, and enforcement referrals generated by Shahidah Williams, her supervisors, or the Compliance Analysis Division detailing how the initial fabricated payoff amounts submitted by City Lending were escalated for regulatory review, and any subsequent disciplinary or enforcement actions taken against the lender.

I request these records be provided in their native, electronic format with all metadata intact. Because these records involve documented regulatory abandonment and subsequent financial harm to a District resident, I expect a comprehensive search that goes beyond a surface-level query.

communications.communication:
This email serves as the City of Detroit’s response to the above-referenced matter. Your request was received at the City of Detroit Law Department Freedom of Information Act Section via facsimile or email on May 6, 2026. Because your request was received by electronic transmission, pursuant to Section 5(1) of the Michigan Freedom of Information Act (the “Act”), MCL 15.235(1); it is deemed to have been received at the Law Department on the next business day, May 7, 2026.

Pursuant to Section 5(2) of the Act, MCL 15.235(2), the City’s response is due within five (5) business days. However, due to the nature and the scope of your request and the volume of the requests received by the City, we are extending the City’s response deadline by ten (10) additional business days in accordance with Section 5(2) (d) of the Act, MCL 15.235(2) (d). Therefore, your request will be granted, denied, or granted in part and denied in part on or before May 28, 2026.

Your request is being handled by Sonia Eckerman. If you have questions regarding your request, or if you did not provide an email address in your request, please forward it to Sonia Eckerman at Sonia.eckerman@detroitmi.gov<mailto:Sonia.eckerman@detroitmi.gov> to provide you a response more readily than by regular mail or fax. Mail and fax are not preferred at this time since they both require in-office support. We thank you in advance for your understanding.

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Raven (Ventura Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Flock Raven records created by the department and Flock Safety
Databases, spreadsheets, and any other documents that provide the following information collectively or individually:
- Number of alerts
- Description, details about the alert (Examples: gunfire, car crash, distress, sideshow)
- List of alert types (Examples: gunfire, car crash, etc)
- Maps and alert locations
- Raven locations
- Response times
- False positives, false negatives, and any record about the technology's accuracy
- Audo recordings of alerts
- Access logs, reason for searches or alerts, audits

Also search documents:
Promotional materials, user guides, and any PowerPoint presentations related to Flock Raven
Contracts and MOU's related to Flock Raven

Policies around Raven and other audio detection technologies
List of agencies your department shares access with and agencies sharing their Raven's with your department 

Please search January 2023 to today's date.
Release files and documents in their original formats in a rolling production.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Ted Turner (CNN)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into Cable News Network founder Robert Edward "Ted" Turner III (November 19, 1938 – May 6, 2026). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/06/business/media/ted-turner-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an associate editor at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest for the Daily Beast, the Asia Times, Responsible Statecraft, the National Interest, America Magazine and the Armenian Weekly, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

**

Upon receipt of your FOIPA request number, you may check the status of your
FOIPA request on the FBI’s electronic FOIA Library (The Vault) on the FBI’s
public website, http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on the ‘Check Status of Your
FOI/PA Request tool’ link. Status updates are performed on a weekly basis. If
you receive a comment that your FOIPA request number was not located in the
database, please check back at a later date.

title: Georgia AG — Travis Johnson / LinkedIn Post Coordination

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia AG — Travis Johnson / LinkedIn Post Coordination
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all communications between Travis Johnson, Chief of Staff for the Office of the Attorney General, and Attorney General Chris Carr — or between Travis Johnson and any other employee of the Office of the Attorney General — regarding either of the following:

1. The LinkedIn post published from the Attorney General's official LinkedIn account on or about April 11, 2026; or

2. Any request by Flock Safety, Bob Carter, or any Flock Safety representative for a like, repost, comment, or other social media engagement from the Attorney General or the Office of the Attorney General.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by email attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

If you contend that any responsive records are exempt from disclosure, please provide a written explanation identifying the specific statutory basis for withholding each record individually.

Please provide records in their original electronic format, including all available metadata.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar
